message.abstract:
<jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Papain-like protease (PL
                  <jats:sup>pro</jats:sup>
                  ) from SARS-CoV-2 is a high-priority target for COVID-19 antiviral drug development. We present protocols for PL
                  <jats:sup>pro</jats:sup>
                  production in
                  <jats:italic>Escherichia coli</jats:italic>
                  . PL
                  <jats:sup>pro</jats:sup>
                  expressed as a fusion with the
                  <jats:italic>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</jats:italic>
                  Smt3 protein (SUMO), is purified and obtained in its native form upon hydrolysis, with yields as high as 38 mg L
                  <jats:sup>-1</jats:sup>
                  . The protocol also provides isotope-enriched samples suitable for NMR studies. Protocols are also presented for PL
                  <jats:sup>pro</jats:sup>
                  characterization by mass spectrometry, 1D
                  <jats:sup>19</jats:sup>
                  F-NMR and 2D heteronuclear NMR, and a fluorescence-based enzyme assay.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Highlights</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        Production, purification, and biochemical analysis of native N- and C-termini PL
                        <jats:sup>pro</jats:sup>
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        High yields in
                        <jats:italic>E. coli</jats:italic>
                        , up to 38 mg L
                        <jats:sup>-1</jats:sup>
                        using lysogeny broth.
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Supports labeled samples for inhibitor interaction studies.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        <jats:sup>19</jats:sup>
                        F NMR and fluorescence assays for inhibitor screening and IC
                        <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                        determination.
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>eTOC Blurb</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    SARS-CoV-2 PL
                    <jats:sup>pro</jats:sup>
                    is a key cysteine protease involved in viral replication and immune evasion, making it an important target for antiviral drug development. This study presents a detailed protocol for PL
                    <jats:sup>pro</jats:sup>
                    production, purification, and biochemical analysis, achieving high yields in
                    <jats:italic>E. coli</jats:italic>
                    . The workflow includes fusion expression with a His-SUMO tag, isotope labeling for inhibitor studies, and assays for screening and quantifying inhibitors. This comprehensive guide facilitates large-scale production of active PL
                    <jats:sup>pro</jats:sup>
                    for drug discovery and structural studies.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title:
From Plasmid to Pure Protein: Production and Characterization of SARS-CoV-2 PL
                  <sup>pro</sup>
